SSPE is a progressive and fatal neurological disorder that can develop years after an individual has contracted measles. The condition results from a persistent measles virus infection in the brain and typically manifests in children and adolescents who were infected with measles at a young age. Recent studies suggest that the incidence of SSPE could be higher than earlier estimates, highlighting the critical importance of measles vaccination to prevent not only the initial infection but also this severe long-term consequence.
